title: Tinea versicolor
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Tinea versicolor is a common superficial fungal infection of the outer skin layer.
It can cause lighter or darker scaly patches, often on the trunk, neck, or upper arms.
It is caused by yeast normally found on skin and is not usually spread person to person.
Extensive, recurrent, immunocompromised, or diagnostically uncertain rashes need clinician-specific handling.
